notes: MITRE operates CWE, the canonical weakness taxonomy referenced by virtually all vulnerability databases. CWE provides a hierarchical classification of software and hardware weaknesses — views (organize by perspective), categories (group related weaknesses), and specific weakness entries. Over 900 weakness types. AI/ML weaknesses added in the CWE-1400 series. Referenced by CVE, NVD, and most vulnerability databases globally.

data.description: The canonical software and hardware weakness taxonomy, operated by MITRE.
data.notes:
Hierarchical taxonomy with three structural levels: views (organize weaknesses by perspective, e.g., CWE-1000 Research Concepts), categories (group related weaknesses, e.g., CWE-19 Data Processing Errors), and weaknesses (specific flaw types, e.g., CWE-79 XSS). Over 900 weakness entries. Referenced by CVE, NVD, and virtually all vulnerability databases.

AI/ML weaknesses added in the CWE-1400 series: CWE-1426 (Improper Validation of Generative AI Output), CWE-1427 (Improper Neutralization of Input for LLM Prompting), CWE-1434 (Insecure ML Model Inference Parameters).

All CWE IDs (views, categories, and weaknesses) use the same numeric format and the same URL template. The CWE number space is shared — no collisions between types. Updated regularly with community contributions.

urls.note: CWE data index — links to XML, CSV, and PDF downloads
Full CWE list in XML format (zipped)

children.description: CWE identifier (e.g., CWE-79, CWE-1427). Covers views, categories, and weaknesses — all use the same ID format.

examples.note: Improper Neutralization of Input for LLM Prompting — AI/ML weakness (CWE-1400 series)
